content:
Hi,

I want to know which setup you use to develop driver and kernel features. Right now, I'm using Ubuntu 25.04 and making a copy of the Linux repository from rust-for-linux. After that, I built and installed the kernel. It's not easy to make changes and test them in the kernel here, because you have to rebuild and install the new artefacts. Do you have any suggestions?
@ManeraKai Type/borrow/... checking is only a build test, and not even a complete one.

Maybe OP is asking for a quicker way to test a new kernel. @Dooraim If you are installing the kernel on your live device, I would instead suggest using a quick VM (assuming the hardware/code you are working on allows for it).
That, or using a second device, is generally a good advice for new development.
A message was moved from this topic to #Networking > `SkBuff` by Miguel Ojeda.
